title: ranunculus subborealis subsp. villosus
intro: Ranunculus subborealis subsp. villosus, also known as the hairy buttercup, is a subspecies of the ranunculus plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The hairy buttercup is known for its medicinal properties, which include being used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, the hairy buttercup is prepared by making a tea from its leaves and flowers, which can be used as a topical treatment or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
